title: Component Pattern Frontmatter (v1)
description: CI-enforced schema for the YAML frontmatter of a component pattern in the SDLC Centre of Excellence. Validated by patterns/_schema/lint and the validate-patterns GitHub Action on every PR that touches a pattern file. LIGHT and advisory: this checks that a pattern is well-formed and cite-able and carries the evidence/validity/sunset metadata the library promises — it does NOT enforce any approval gate. A human PR review is what blesses a pattern; this schema only makes sure the human has the fields they need to do it. Forbids the computed fields (maturity, adoption_count) as inputs because they are derived from the adoption ledger, never authored.

pattern_key.description: Stable, cite-able key that survives renames. Cited by the build bundle, retrieval and every superseding/superseded reference. UPPER-KEBAB after the PAT- prefix, e.g. PAT-WEBAPP-PG.

name.description: Human-readable pattern name, e.g. 'Three-tier web app on managed Postgres'.

category.description: Top-level family the pattern belongs to.

intent.description: One sentence in 'Use this WHEN ... so that ...' form. The retrieval anchor and embedding seed — make it discriminating, not generic.

deployment_topology.description: Where the components run, in a phrase, e.g. 'single-region managed containers + managed Postgres'.

data_placement.description: Where data lives / residency shape, in a phrase, e.g. 'in-region only; no cross-region replication'.

summary.description: A short paragraph: what the pattern is, at a glance. The body of the pattern file carries the full Context/Solution/Tradeoffs.

approval_status.description: Lifecycle state, set by a human in PR review. 'candidate' = proposed, not yet reviewed. 'provisional' = reviewed, usable with care, limited evidence. 'approved' = reviewed and blessed for fast-track reuse. 'deprecated' = retired; do not adopt, see superseded_by. No agent ever sets this — it is changed only via a human-reviewed PR.

valid_from.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the pattern became valid / was first authored.

validity_check_months.description: How often (in months) the pattern should be re-checked for currency. Used to derive a review-by date from the last validation. Defaults to 12 if omitted.

sunset_at.description: Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which the pattern should be considered for retirement even if not yet superseded. Advisory: surfaces in the library as 'sunsetting', never auto-removes.

supersedes.description: Pattern_keys this pattern replaces (the forward half of the deprecation chain). Each entry must be a valid pattern_key.

superseded_by.description: The pattern_key that replaces this one. REQUIRED when approval_status = deprecated (a deprecated pattern must name its successor so adopters are never dead-ended). A valid pattern_key.

approved_by.description: Who blessed the pattern in PR review (name or GitHub handle). REQUIRED when approval_status is provisional or approved. A net-new human-owned fact.

approved_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the human approval. REQUIRED when approval_status is provisional or approved.

evidence.description: Artefacts proving the pattern was actually BUILT — not just imagined. Links to a repo, PR, runbook, ADR, dashboard, post-mortem, or load-test report. REQUIRED (at least one) when approval_status is provisional or approved: the library only fast-tracks reuse of things with evidence behind them.

title.description: What the artefact is, e.g. 'Prod deploy of Citizen Services on this topology'.

url.description: Link to the artefact (repo, PR, runbook, dashboard, doc).

project.description: Optional: the engagement/project the artefact came from (provenance 'first proven on X').

date.description: Optional ISO date the artefact was produced.

attached_nfrs.description: The typed, propagatable NFRs this pattern carries — the heart of the reuse payoff. On adopt, each becomes a derivable requirement in a downstream project. Every NFR must carry a kind, a statement, and an acceptance_criterion (the testable 'done' that makes it inspectable, not just an assertion).

kind.description: The NFR family. The 11 governed kinds.

nfr_key.description: Optional stable key for the NFR, e.g. NFR-TLS-TRANSIT — becomes a req_key prefix on propagation.

statement.description: What the NFR asserts, e.g. 'TLS 1.2+ in transit between tiers and to managed Postgres'.

acceptance_criterion.description: The testable 'done', e.g. 'All inter-tier connections present a cert chain to TLS>=1.2; verified by scan'. Carried verbatim into the downstream requirement on adopt.

measurable_target.description: Optional concrete target, e.g. '99.9% monthly' or '<=200ms p95'.

is_waivable.description: Optional: can a downstream project waive this NFR? Waiving a non-waivable NFR is a recorded compromise. Defaults to true if omitted.

constraints.description: What the pattern ENFORCES — the things you give up by adopting it. Each is the fuel a downstream re-validation replays. 'hard' = cannot be waived; 'soft' = a waiver is a recorded compromise.

statement.description: What the pattern forces, e.g. 'All writes are async via the event bus; no synchronous DB writes'.

enforced.description: 'hard' = cannot be waived. 'soft' = a waiver is a recorded compromise.

degrades_capability.description: Optional machine hint matched against a requirement's capability tags, e.g. 'instant-write', 'real-time', 'cross-region'.

fulfils.description: Optional back-reference: the capability_keys this pattern fulfils, so a reader on the pattern sees the need it serves. The forward edge lives on the capability (fulfilled_by[].pattern_key); this is the optional mirror. Each entry is a capability_key (UPPER-KEBAB after CAP-). Additive — a pattern without this field still validates.

maturity.description: FORBIDDEN as an input. maturity (battle-tested / emerging / experimental) is COMPUTED from the adoption ledger, never authored in frontmatter. Remove it from this file.

adoption_count.description: FORBIDDEN as an input. adoption_count ('used in N engagements') is COMPUTED from the adoption ledger, never authored in frontmatter. Remove it from this file.

allOf.$comment: approval_status in {provisional, approved} requires the human approval trail + at least one piece of build evidence.
A deprecated pattern must name its successor so adopters are never dead-ended.
